hero.ariaLabel: A forest habitat with plants and animals

heroCopy.lede: This page is designed like a premium educational website — but when you print it, it becomes a clean worksheet. Read each section, study the images, then answer in the writing boxes (type on screen or write on paper).
heroCopy.whatStudentsLearn: In this lesson, students explore what makes something a living thing, what plants and animals need to survive, how habitats provide resources, and how adaptations help living things solve problems in their environment. Each section includes quick facts, a “look closely” mission, and a writing prompt that builds science vocabulary and complete-sentence explanations.
learningTargets: I can describe clues that show something is living.
I can compare what plants need and what animals need.
I can explain what a habitat provides (food, water, shelter, space).
I can connect an adaptation to a habitat challenge.
studentDirections: Read the section and study the image.
Use the “Quick Facts” to support your answers.
Write in complete sentences when asked.
On paper: you may circle, label, or underline key details.

keyVocabulary.def: something that grows, uses energy, and needs resources.
resources required to survive (water, energy, food, air, space).
the place a plant or animal lives.
a body part or behavior that helps survival.

sections.title: What makes something a living thing?
sections.lede: Living things have needs and can do certain jobs to stay alive. Many living things grow, use energy, respond to changes, and make more of their kind.

cards.title: Quick Facts
items: Plants make their own food using sunlight.
Animals must eat plants or other animals.
All living things need water and energy.

cards.title: Look Closely
items: Find a leaf, stem, and flower (or bud).
What clues show the plant is alive?

prompt.label: Prompt A — Living or Not?
prompt.placeholder: Explain how you know the thing in the picture is alive. Use at least two clues.
prompt.teacherKey: ✔ Key idea: Living things grow, use energy, respond to changes, and reproduce (plants also make food from sunlight).

sections.title: What do plants and animals need?
sections.lede: Plants and animals both need water and energy. Animals also need food. Plants need sunlight to make food. Many animals need air to breathe.
sections.callout: Website on screen, worksheet on paper: these writing boxes work both ways.

prompt.label: Prompt B — Compare
prompt.placeholder:
1) One need plants and animals share is: ______________________
2) One need that is different is: _____________________________
prompt.teacherKey: ✔ Key idea: Shared needs include water/energy; difference: plants need sunlight to make food, animals must eat.

sections.imageQuery: plants animals need food water air

sections.title: Habitats: a living thing’s home
sections.lede: A habitat is the place where a plant or animal lives. A good habitat provides food, water, shelter, and space.

cards.title: Image Mission
items: On paper: circle where shelter might be.

prompt.label: Prompt C — Habitat Report
prompt.placeholder: Describe this habitat. What resources could help plants and animals survive here?
prompt.teacherKey: ✔ Key idea: Habitat provides resources: food, water, shelter, and space (use visible evidence).

sections.title: Adaptations: helpful body parts or behaviors
sections.lede: An adaptation is something that helps a living thing survive. It can be a body part (like sharp claws) or a behavior (like migrating). Adaptations match the habitat.

cards.title: Examples
items: Waxy leaves help plants save water.

cards.title: Think Like a Scientist
items: What problem does the habitat create?

prompt.label: Prompt D — Match & Explain
prompt.placeholder: Choose one plant OR one animal. Name one adaptation and explain how it helps in its habitat.
prompt.teacherKey: ✔ Key idea: Adaptation = trait/behavior that improves survival (tie it to habitat problem + solution).

sections.title: Quick Check + Create
sections.lede: Finish strong. Use the words needs, habitat, and adaptation.

cards.title: Mini Quiz
items: Define habitat in your own words.
Name 2 needs all living things share.

promptE.label: Prompt E — Summary
promptE.placeholder: Write a short summary: needs, habitats, and adaptations. Use all three words.
promptE.teacherKey: ✔ Key idea: Summary includes shared needs (water/energy), habitat resources, and adaptations that match habitat.

promptF.label: Prompt F — Create an Animal
promptF.teacherKey: ✔ Key idea: Adaptations must connect to habitat challenges (cold, heat, predators, low water, etc.).
